description: You are a bioinformatics scientist evaluating cross-species conservation of cytochrome c as a mitochondrial biomarker for drug toxicity assays. Perform a quantitative comparison of the 8 cytochrome c protein sequences found in ~/UGENE_Data/cytochrome_c_multispecies.fasta using UGENE. Conduct a multiple sequence alignment using the MUSCLE algorithm and generate a pairwise identity/distance matrix. Export the resulting alignment to ~/UGENE_Data/results/cytc_alignment.sto in Stockholm format and the matrix to ~/UGENE_Data/results/cytc_distance_matrix.csv. Document your findings in a report at ~/UGENE_Data/results/cytc_analysis_report.txt, including the sequence count, alignment length, the most similar and most divergent species pairs with their percentages, the percent identity between Human and all other species, and a conclusion on which animal model has the most conserved cytochrome c relative to humans.

metadata.data_source: UniProt cytochrome c protein sequences for 8 species
